transcript.clean_text: sunny skies greeted the riders at the start of stage seven of the gyro d'italia and the first hungarian ever to wear pink attila walter led the judo after another rainy mountain stage today should be an opportunity for the sprinters 181 kilometers leaving notre esco heading south through abruzzo a brief incursion in land to kierti for a king of the mountains points craic you're for the intermediate sprint and then back to the coast the adriatic coast hitting more easy and finishing in termole first finish in the town for 15 years and 181 kilometers of riding to do when the flag went three teams attacked three riders attacked and there was no reaction behind it the sprinters and the gc teams happy simon polo joined by umberto morengo and mark christian the three italian teams invited to the race alton in the breakaway kt showed itself off as usual and at the king of the mountain spread it would be seymour polo the rider from switzerland who'd go off and take the three maximum points christian second umberto marengo in third breakaway had a maximum gap of just over five minutes but they were never really given too much leeway as the afternoon went on the sprinters wanting to make sure that before a weekend of mountain climbing there'd be an opportunity to take victory there was a climb to the intermediate sprint at crechio the sprint fought out in front and taken by maringo that movement behind has let peter segan in with the opportunity still looking for points in that maya chikamino competition and picking up valuable points he'd then wait to see if he could win the stage weather clouded over as the afternoon went on and the riders continue to go south second intermediate sprint was hard affording the breakaway it was won by maringo pulo giving is all not able to do anything and a calm day first day for the 22 year old falter leading the judo being well looked after by his cool farmer fdg teammates it would be just outside 15 kilometers to go when the breakaway would be caught riding into a block headwind not really helping their cause gc teams wanting to stay safe sprint teams desperate for position as the riders will be racing into a shop right-hander with one and a half kilometers to go and an uphill finish attacks from eolo were well marked hugh carthy suffered a mechanical on the way up and inside the final 500 meters there'd be a surprise difficult to spot at the time which rider was going milano's been hanging about but it'd be fernando cavillia who would go out desperately trying to go for a long sprint the reaction came from caleb yuen already a winner two days before this she will lie on his wheel has gone on up there as well ewan would hit the front and caleb yoon nobody would get around him his 50th career win lotto sudal's 30th gino d'italia stage win ewen beating chimolai and merlier with musketeer and pascual on in the top five caleb you and under the podium again a very good jira d'italia so far and attila walter keeping the general classification lead 11 seconds his advantage over renko even upon 16 in front of the tour winner from two years ago megan verna what's next stage eight leaving for ja and puglia and going to guardia san francisco couple of big climbs along the way and an uphill finish a three-star stage another medium mountain day boca de la silva the biggest test for the uphill finish interesting as it might cause plenty of splits also bonus seconds available right at the bottom of it stage eight promises to be a good one another weekend of uphill fighting for pink you
